pattern: A most-constrained-first greedy threads every ordered pairing into a sequence where no team appears on back-to-back days.
hints: Each team must meet every other team home and away, giving n times n minus one matches with one per day.
The only blocked choice each day is reusing either team that played the day before, so prefer matches between the teams that still have the most games left.

Repeatedly pick the remaining pairing whose two teams have the largest combined number of outstanding matches and that avoids yesterday's two teams; very small team counts simply have no valid arrangement.

edge_inputs.description: two teams cannot avoid consecutive play
four teams is the largest impossible case
five teams is the smallest feasible case

stress.description: sweep a range of team counts including infeasible and large feasible ones
stress.note: single integer argument; validator checks any valid schedule
